title: Fix bug in calculating maximum frame size

issues.body: I believe this be `self.backend.create_event`, is that right @florimondmanca?

issues.body: Correct! We should use methods on the backend instead of calling asyncio operations directly. :)

issues.body:
Instead of the section here I think we can use `self.h2_state.acknowledge_received_data(event.flow_controlled_length, stream_id)`?

We don't need anything beyond the `h2_state.streams[stream_id].open`, it's all handled by `acknowledge_received_data()`.
We should revert this change

issues.body: I'm not sure that here we need to reduce the value of `max_outbound_frame_size` by one, because according to the `h2` documentation, this is the [maximum value](https://python-hyper.org/projects/h2/en/stable/api.html#h2.connection.H2Connection.max_outbound_frame_size) that can be sent and it [should

issues.body: I think that we should replace `len(event.data)` with `event.flow_controlled_length`, according to [this](https://python-hyper.org/projects/h2/en/stable/advanced-usage.html#working-with-flow-control) part of the h2 documentation, and I also don’t quite understand why the code below with manual contr
I think that `KeyError` can be raised here, if HTTP/2 on the server will not work correctly. Perhaps we should check that `event_stream_id` value is in the `window_update_received` dictionary.

issues.body: In testing an error was thrown when chunk size was `self.h2_state.max_outbound_frame_size` (which appeared to always be 2^14 = 16384) which said that the limit on frame size was the 16383. Adding in the -1 fixed the issue, and I assumed that somewhere the maximum value was being interpreted as stric

issues.body: I think that this error was caused by unhandled `WindowsUpdated` event and error in splitting data into chunks. In `h2` this [checked](https://github.com/python-hyper/hyper-h2/blob/385ced7d30a7ccb9b3f163f1bc4e90cf2f4ee184/h2/connection.py#L850) as greater than, so I don't think that there is a need

issues.body: Why do we still need these 2 lines? As I see it `.receive_event` will process all write operations inside itself, which means that message about increasing the size of the window will be included in the sending payload. So if the stream is open, then the data will definitely be sent there or even ea

issues.body: Can we group these two tests together, like other sync/async pairs? Also the convention in this file seems to be test_async_... for the async test cases. :)

Just a comprehension note — I’m wondering why this condition indicates that we should wait for the window to be updated? Is it happening when the max outbound limit is 0? Or when we receive no data?

Maybe a link to an appropriate section of the HTTP/2 standard could be useful here.
